sourceMovie.title: Shinjuku Incident
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Shinjuku Incident (2009) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Extortion
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Shinjuku Incident clicked for you, Extortion (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Shinjuku Incident?
faq.answer: Try Extortion, SPL 2: A Time for Consequences, The Jackal—each shares DNA with Shinjuku Incident while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Shinjuku Incident?
faq.answer: Shinjuku Incident (2009) is best described as Drama, Action, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Shinjuku Incident worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
